title: [suggestion]allow the mix of ANSI and Non-ANSI module declaration
body:
I don't know if it's reasonable. Actually I think users should follow SV LRM.
but many designers writes the non-stardard code. and all the tools compromises.

Here is the coding.

```verilog

module top(input clk, output [31:0] cnt);

reg [31:0] cnt;

always @(posedge clk) begin
    cnt <= cnt + 1;
end

endmodule
```

compile it with VCS,  Xcelium, or check with Spyglass. 
All tools will give out the warning.

Could Verilator give out the warning instead of error as well?
